title: Containers log acquisition API become slow and memory-consuming after periodical calls.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

My product call APIs of `GET /containers/{container id}/logs?follow=1&since={time of previous call}&stderr=1&stdout=1%tail=all` periodically. (it might be a not good implementation, but as it is now...)  
If we run the product about ten days, the duration becomes longer and it takes over 4 hours, and podman uses a lot of memory (1.0 GB for physical memory and 2.5 GB for virtual memory).  
If I set the podman's loglevel as DEBUG and the connection number in IdleTracker log increased about 20 more.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run about 40 containers

2. Call container's log acquisition API periodically, about 1 times per 3 minutes.

3. Walt about 10 days.

**Describe the results you received:**

The API calls takes about 4 hours and podman uses a lot of memory.

Extraction from `/var/log/messages`

```
May 18 17:43:09 localhost.localdomain podman[3320]: "time=2022-05-18T17:43:09-04:00" level=info msg="APIHandler(c8f950ef-a7a8-40a3-b34d-5ee8ea53a652) -- GET /containers/... BEGIN"
May 18 22:01:51 localhost.localdomain podman[3320]: "time=2022-05-18T22:01:51-04:00" level=debug msg="APIHandler(c8f950ef-a7a8-40a3-b34d-5ee8ea53a652) -- GET /containers/... END"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

- The APIs finish about 3 or 4 minutes, same time as a little later after my product service starts.
- The memory usage of podman does not change.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@localhost log]# podman version
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Sep 21 04:41:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@localhost log]#
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[root@localhost log]# podman info --debug
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["machine"] from "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf".
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@localhost log]# rpm -q podman
podman-3.3.1-9.module+el8.5.0+12697+018f24d7.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
The RHEL server is a virtual machine runs on ESXi 7.0.

comments.body: Can you test this with podman v4.1 please?

thanks for advice, but we can't try with podman v4.1 instantly, as some API client we use does not follow API spec changes.

Do you have any fixed issue idea about logs API to solve this problem?

Our team found that the periodical API call of getting container's log cause the issue and the minimum reproduce procedure.
This report might not be to the point, so that I close it and recreate new one.
